title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stomach pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with stomach pain because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken orally, the tincture coats the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach ulcers, acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, licorice root has natural antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax the muscles of the stomach and reduce cramping and discomfort associated with digestive disorders.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound that soothes inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting. Its anti-inflammatory effects help calm the digestive system.
Licoric acid: A flavonoid that has been shown to have antacid properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn and indigestion, thereby alleviating stomach pain.

Amino acids: Licorice root contains several amino acids, including glycine, proline, and glutamic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive tract, further helping to alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce stomach pain by inhibiting the production of stomach acid, thereby alleviating inflammation and irritation.
Licoric acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the stomach by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Isoliquiritigenin: A flavonoid that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce stomach pain by protecting the stomach lining from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for medicinal purposes, as they contain high levels of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe stomach inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used as a secondary part, offering additional benefits due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which complement the effects of the roots.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used in tincture form, providing further anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that contribute to alleviating stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity.
Wash the dried roots to remove impurities and excess debris in cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the washed roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Steep the chopped roots in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove the roots and obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baby rubber plant tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The plant's extracts work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with stomach cramps, bloating, and gas. By calming the stomach muscles, baby rubber plant tinctures can also help to ease colic symptoms in infants, promoting a more peaceful and comfortable sleep for both baby and parent.
remedy_properties: Soothing Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Baby Rubber Plant tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach, providing relief from stomach pain.

Gastric Soothing and Protective Effects: The tincture's saponins and other compounds may help soothe and protect the gastric mucosa, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by acid reflux or other stomach conditions, which can contribute to stomach pain.

Antispasmodic Properties: The alkaloids present in Baby Rubber Plant tincture have a calming effect on smooth muscle contractions, including those in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Peperomia obtusifolia, such as piperic acid, may help alleviate stomach pain due to their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in the plant, including piperic acid derivatives, might contribute to stomach pain relief by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and compounds.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids such as ferulic acid, present in Peperomia obtusifolia, may help reduce stomach pain due to their antioxidant properties, which can mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for stomach pain due to their purported anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for stomach pain due to their purported ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems are used to make tinctures for stomach pain due to their purported ability to relax smooth muscle and alleviate cramping in the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh peperomia obtusifolia leaves at 10 am on a sunny day.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with filtered water for 5 minutes.
Chop 50g of the cleaned leaves into small pieces and place them in a glass jar.
Steep the chopped leaves in 500ml of 50% vodka at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, methyl salicylate, works similarly to aspirin, reducing pain and inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, wintergreen's soothing properties help calm stomach cramps and spasms, providing fast relief from discomfort. By addressing the root cause of the pain, herbal wintergreen tinctures promote a sense of relaxation and ease, allowing for improved digestion and overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: This key compound in wintergreen tincture has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective at relieving stomach pain and cramps due to its ability to reduce inflammation and numb pain receptors.

Gaultherin: Wintergreen's unique antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, Gaultherin, helps soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, alleviating stomach pain and discomfort associated with conditions like IBS or gastritis.

Menthol: This aromatic compound found in wintergreen tincture has a calming effect on the mucous membranes, providing relief from stomach acid reflux, heartburn, and nausea by reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract.
remedy_constituents: Gaultherin: Gaultherin, a sesquiterpene lactone, is a key anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, particularly peduncoside, are bitter compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and promote gastric motility, helping to alleviate stomach cramps and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Gaultheria procumbens tinctures possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of methyl salicylate, a compound that provides relief from stomach pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are also widely used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to soothe stomach pain and cramps.
Barks: The barks of Gaultheria procumbens are used to create a tincture that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain due to their salicylate content and astringent properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems by cutting them with scissors when they are in full growth.
Dry 1 part of the harvested gaultheria procumbens in a paper bag or dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part of the dried gaultheria procumbens with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking and no exposure to direct sunlight.
Strain and bottle the resulting tincture using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove the gaultheria procumbens solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with stomach pain because it is a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Its bitter properties stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing bloating and cramps. Additionally, dandelion's ability to promote liver function can help to eliminate toxins from the body, which may be contributing to stomach pain. By promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing inflammation, herbal dandelion tinctures can provide relief for individuals suffering from stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Bitters: Dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate digestive enzymes and improve liver function, which can help alleviate stomach pain caused by poor digestion or liver congestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory flavonoids that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can ease stomach pain caused by conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, or ulcerative colitis.

Mucilages: Dandelion's mucilage-rich sap forms a protective barrier on the stomach lining, soothing and calming inflammation, irritation, and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for stomach pain caused by acid reflux, ulcers, or other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin, Taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe stomach pain.

Taraxol: A triterpene saponin, Taraxol has been shown to have gastroprotective effects by reducing gastric acid secretion and increasing the production of mucus in the stomach, thereby providing relief from stomach pain.

Apigenin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, Apigenin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, providing relief from stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of inulin, which can help soothe stomach inflammation and aid digestion.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach pain and reduce inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help relieve stomach cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in late summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots and leaves with 60-80 percent ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with stomach pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The gel of the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to prevent infections that can contribute to stomach pain. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, herbal aloe tinctures provide effective relief from stomach pain and promote digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach pain.
Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and pain.

Soothing and protective mucilage: The mucilage found in aloe vera gel forms a soothing, protective barrier on the stomach lining, calming irritation and reducing discomfort caused by digestive issues like heartburn or ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: A anthraquinone glycoside that helps with stomach pain by stimulating bowel movements and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Aloe-emodin: A anthraquinone that helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can cause digestive issues.
Saponins: A type of triterpenoid glycoside that helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes in the digestive tract from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for tinctures, as they contain a high concentration of aloe-emodin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach pain.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain a compound called aloin, which has been used to treat stomach pain and inflammation by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion.
Roots: The roots of Aloe vera contain a compound called aloe-erythroside, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 percent organic aloe vera leaves and a glass jar with a lid that can be sterilized.
Chop 200 grams of aloe vera leaves into small pieces and place them in the jar.
Add 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka to the jar and seal it immediately.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with stomach pain because it contains compounds like quassin and limonin, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, soothing cramps and discomfort associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis. By calming the gut lining and reducing spasms, Jesuit's bark tinctures provide relief from stomach pain and discomfort, allowing for improved digestion and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Cinchona alkaloids: Specifically, quinine and quinidine, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to relieve stomach pain by reducing inflammation and numbing pain receptors.
Volatile oils: Containing compounds like caffeic acid and cinnamic acid, these oils help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach pain.

Flavonoids: Jesuit's bark tincture is rich in flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by inflammation or acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: A well-known alkaloid in Cinchona officinalis, quinine helps alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation and relieving muscle spasms in the digestive tract.
Quinidine: Another alkaloid present in Cinchona officinalis, quinidine has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach pain and reduce swelling in the digestive system.
Cinchonine: A secondary alkaloid found in Cinchona officinalis, cinchonine has a direct impact on the stomach muscles, helping to relax them and reduce cramps and spasms that cause stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain quinine, a natural compound that helps alleviate stomach pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks are rich in alkaloids, including quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to soothe stomach pain.
Roots: The roots contain a mixture of alkaloids and glycosides that help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in the spring or fall when the plant is dormant for 30 minutes.
Dry the harvested bark in a well-ventilated area at 30°c for 2 weeks.
Chop 200 grams of dried bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar for 30 minutes.
Combine 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol with the chopped bark in the jar and steep for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tannins present in fennel help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the gut, alleviating cramps and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, further reducing stomach pain and discomfort. By promoting relaxation of the muscles in the digestive system, fennel tinctures provide relief from stomach pain and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), indigestion, and other digestive disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like limonene and anethole, which reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as cramping and bloating.

Gastric relaxant properties: The essential oils in fennel tincture have a soothing effect on the stomach muscles, helping to relax the digestive system and relieve spasms and cramps associated with indigestion, nausea, and vomiting.

Carminative properties: Fennel tincture contains carminative compounds that help to eliminate gas and bloating from the body by increasing digestion and relieving pressure on the stomach and intestines.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone is a monoterpene alcohol that helps with stomach pain by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing inflammation.
Anethole: Anethole is an aromatic ether that helps with stomach pain by reducing spasms and inflammation in the digestive tract, and also by inhibiting the release of stomach acid.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid is a phenolic compound that helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, and also by protecting the stomach lining from damage.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils and active compounds like anethole, which provide carminative and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves contain compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe stomach pain and inflammation.
Fruits: The fruits of Foeniculum vulgare, also known as seeds, contain anethole, which helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach pain and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning to capture optimal essential oil content.
Clean the harvested material and chop it into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped material with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part foeniculum vulgare to 2 parts vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture from solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to calm the muscles in the stomach and intestines, alleviating cramps, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid or inflammation, further contributing to its pain-relieving effects on digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain such as cramps and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes: Ginger has been shown to stimulate digestive enzyme production, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help alleviate stomach pain caused by indigestion, bloating, and gas.

Gastroprotective properties: Ginger's bioactive compounds have been found to possess gastroprotective effects, helping to protect the mucous lining of the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux, ulcers, and other digestive conditions, thereby reducing stomach pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating stomach pain.

Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce pain and inflammation in the stomach, as well as relax the muscles in the digestive tract.

Volatiles (specifically, gingerene and bisabolol): Volatiles, including gingerene and bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate stomach pain.
Roots: As the primary source of rhizome tissue, roots of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to extract the beneficial properties for stomach pain relief.
Leaves: Although less commonly used than rhizomes, the leaves of Zingiber officinale contain some of the same active compounds and are used in some herbal preparations for stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning after the plant has completed its growth cycle.
Clean and peel the rhizomes then chop them into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their natural oils.
Combine 1 cup of chopped rhizomes with 2 cups of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create the tincture base.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle then discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a key component in turmeric, reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The bioactive compound also has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, turmeric tinctures can effectively soothe and calm the digestive system, providing fast relief from stomach pain and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach pain.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the stomach lining, reducing the risk of stomach pain and inflammation.

Gastroprotective: Turmeric has been shown to have gastroprotective effects by reducing gastric acid secretion and mucosal damage, which can help soothe and protect the stomach from irritation and pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the activity of digestive enzymes, thereby providing relief from digestive discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Demethoxycurcumin, another polyphenolic compound, helps soothe stomach pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to inflammation in the digestive tract.

Turmerone: Turmerone, a sesquiterpene, helps alleviate stomach pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and modulate the activity of digestive enzymes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high content of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain.
Roots: Utilized for their bioactive compounds, including curcuminoids, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Barks: Employed due to their containments of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa rhizomes when the plant is in flower stage or shortly after.
Chop the harvested rhizomes into small pieces weighing 500 grams to ensure adequate quantity.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 80 percent ethanol by volume in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days to facilitate extraction.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solid particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce spasms. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach and intestines, easing cramps and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects help alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers for stomach pain. As a result, herbal peppermint tinctures provide quick relief from stomachaches, making it a natural and effective remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and menthone, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of stomach pain such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort.

Digestive stimulants: The volatile oils present in peppermint tincture, including menthol and limonene, have been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and vomiting by increasing the movement of food through the digestive system.

Spasmolytic effects: Peppermint's menthol content has a natural ability to relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramps, spasms, and other symptoms associated with stomach pain caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), dyspepsia, or acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps with stomach pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate cramps and spasms.
Menthone: It has a localized anesthetic effect, numbing the stomach and surrounding areas to reduce pain and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid (a type of phenolic compound): It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Highly concentrated in menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties.
Stems: Contain menthol and menthone, which help relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate cramps.
Roots: Rich in menthol and menthone, which have a soothing effect on the stomach and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for solvent absorption.
Combine 1 cup of the chopped plant material with 2 cups of 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow for maximum solvent extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with stomach pain because its soothing properties gently calm digestive discomfort. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating cramps, bloating, and spasms that often accompany irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, chamomile's natural sedative effects help to relax the muscles in the stomach, promoting a sense of calm and reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in chamomile, like bisabolol and farnesene, have a calming effect on smooth muscle tissue, relaxing the digestive system and reducing spasms that may be contributing to stomach pain.

Soothing effects on the gut lining: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes help to calm and protect the mucous membranes in the gut, reducing irritation and inflammation, which can help alleviate stomach pain, cramps, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are rich in bioactive compounds, making them ideal for creating a soothing and calming tincture to alleviate stomach pain.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, making their inclusion in a tincture effective in reducing stomach discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried matricaria chamomilla flowers 1 part in a clean glass jar to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Measure one cup of vodka or rum as the solvent and pour it over the dried herbs to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for four weeks in a dark place with occasional shaking allowing the herbs to release their active compounds.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place taking note of the preparation date and recommended dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in maypop, such as passionflower and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and spasms in the gut, providing quick relief from cramps and discomfort. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce bloating and gas, allowing for smoother digestion and a more balanced gut environment. remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Maypop's anxiolytic compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to stomach pain by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
Spasmolytic properties: The spasmolytic effects of Maypop tinctures help relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are valued for their ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
Fruits: Maypop fruits, also known as passionfruits, contain antispasmodic compounds that help calm stomach cramps and spasms associated with stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Combine 100g of dried flower material with 500ml of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for at least four weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container after the four-week period.
Bottle the final tincture and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its soothing properties, which help to calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief from discomfort and cramping. Additionally, marshmallow tincture forms a protective barrier on the lining of the gut, shielding it from further irritation and allowing the natural healing process to take place. This holistic approach promotes healthy digestion and alleviates stomach pain associated with conditions such as IBS, acid reflux, and gastritis.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier in the digestive tract, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce swelling and pain in the stomach and digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of conditions like IBS and gastritis.

Soften mucus and aid digestion: Marshmallow tincture's mucilages also help to soften and loosen excess mucus in the digestive tract, making it easier to pass stool and reducing constipation, while also aiding digestion by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, which provides soothing and protective properties for the digestive tract.
Bark: The bark contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow roots althaea officinalis in the early morning, ensuring they are free of contaminants and damage.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces weigh them and measure their quantity accurately, using approximately 100 grams per batch.
Combine the chopped roots with a neutral-tasting vodka or ethanol at a 1:5 ratio by weight steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for eight weeks.
Shake the jar daily during the steeping period to facilitate maximum extraction of bioactive compounds monitor the mixture's color and consistency, which should become darker and thicker over time.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids and store the tincture in a dark place for at least two months before use.

Antacid and Antispasmodic: The tincture's antacid properties can help neutralize stomach acid and alleviate heartburn and indigestion, while its antispasmodic effects can calm muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing pain and cramping.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect the mucous membranes of the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Pot Marigold are used in traditional medicine due to their bitter and astringent properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues such as stomach pain.
Seeds: The seeds of Pot Marigold contain carotenoids and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn on a dry day when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in small batches using a food dehydrator set at 95°f for 8 hours or until brittle.
Grind 200 grams of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered calendula with 500 milliliters of 90% ethanol and let it steep in a dark glass container for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach pain.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of stomach pain and discomfort.

Softpellicular mucilage production: Kansas Coneflower contains a type of mucilage that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing a physical barrier against irritants and inflammation that can cause stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for stomach pain relief.

Fruits: The fruits contain a unique combination of antioxidants and flavonoids that can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce inflammation, providing natural relief from stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall season for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop the harvested roots into small pieces and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine one part chopped echinacea root with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, filling to the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily during the infusion process.
After the infusion period strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and cramps. Additionally, kansas coneflower has natural antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with stomach pain.

Fenugreek tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The herb's mucilages and saponins work to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with cramps, bloating, and gas. Additionally, fenugreek's prebiotic properties promote a healthy gut microbiome, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders. By addressing these underlying causes, fenugreek tinctures provide natural relief from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by conditions like gastritis and ulcers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as galactomannan and flavonoids, inhibit prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators, providing relief from stomach pain, cramps, and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Gastroprotective properties: Fenugreek tincture's mucilages and saponins create a protective barrier on the stomach lining, shielding it from acid reflux, heartburn, and other digestive irritants that can cause pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and alleviating stomach pain.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain galactomannan, a natural fiber that helps soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fenugreek leaves and stems in early morning, ensuring sufficient quantity for tincture preparation.
Wash the harvested plant material thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris, then air-dry it completely.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using scissors or a food processor, ensuring uniform size for efficient extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped fenugreek material with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly and storing it in a cool dark place for 6 weeks.

Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and transferring the liquid tincture to a darker colored glass container for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds present in elder berries, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach discomforts. Additionally, elder tinctures can help calm nervous digestive issues, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing abdominal cramps and spasms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Elder tincture helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause stomach pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture has anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the digestive tract lining and contribute to stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing stomach pain and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help to calm digestive issues and ease cramps, making it a popular choice for stomach pain remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers at full bloom in the morning.
Cleanly chop the harvested flowers into smaller pieces for easier extraction.
Combine 1 part sambucus nigra flowers with 1 part vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a ratio.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and date the finished tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram tinctures helps with stomach pain because its natural compounds, such as thymol and borneol, have a soothing effect on the digestive system. They help to calm inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing discomfort and cramping. Additionally, wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach disorders, promoting a sense of relief and relaxation for those experiencing abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Wild marjoram tincture helps relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the gut lining, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort caused by conditions like IBS or gastritis.
Carminative property: The tincture helps expel gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion and discomfort associated with stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Wild Marjoram contain a higher concentration of essential oils, which are responsible for its digestive and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating stomach pain.

Leaves: The leaves of Wild Marjoram have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including indigestion, nausea, and bloating, due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is at its highest potency.
Wash the collected herbs thoroughly with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine 250 grams of dried or 500 grams of fresh origanum vulgare with 1250 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the herbs have infused their flavor and medicine into the liquid.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated digestive tracts. The citral and geraniol compounds found in lemongrass have a natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort and pain. Additionally, lemongrass has been shown to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, making it a popular natural remedy for stomach pain relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain such as cramps and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in lemongrass tinctures, including citral and limonene, have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissues in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate spasms and cramping that can contribute to stomach pain.

Carminative properties: Lemongrass has natural carminative properties, which means it helps to eliminate gas and reduce bloating, alleviating pressure on the stomach and reducing symptoms of discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of lemongrass have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of lemongrass, the rhyzome is used to treat digestive issues and reduces inflammation in the gut.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves at dawn when dewy and store in a clean paper bag for later use.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator set to 60°c for 6 hours or air-drying for several days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure uniform extraction.
Mix one part cymbopogon citratus powder with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and store it at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture for future use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stomach pain are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, used to soothe and calm digestive issues. These natural remedies help alleviate stomach pain by reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and calming the gut-brain connection. Examples of herbal tinctures that can provide relief include peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience improved digestion, reduced nausea, and a sense of calm, ultimately enhancing their quality of life and allowing them to live with greater comfort and well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. Herbal tinctures like peppermint, ginger, and dandelion root can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other conditions, like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and menstrual cramps, may also be alleviated with herbal tinctures, promoting relaxation and relief from symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with stomach pain is a blend of ginger, peppermint, and licorice root. Ginger helps to reduce inflammation and ease nausea, while peppermint soothes the digestive system and calms cramps. Licorice root, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, shields the stomach lining and promotes healing. When taken together, these tinctures can provide fast relief from stomach discomfort, making them a popular natural remedy for digestive issues. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, five plant remedies were evaluated for their anti-ulcerogenic potency in treating stomach ache. The methods used in the study involved preparing decoctions or methanol extracts from the plants mentioned, based on their traditional application methods, and then testing these extracts on an ethanol-induced gastric ulcer model in rats. The results found that all the plant extracts showed significant stomach protection against the ulcer model, with pharmacological experiments and histopathological evaluations confirming these effects.
